title: wire:sort
description: Livewire provides drag-and-drop sorting through the wire:sort directive. Add it to a parent element and use wire:sort:item on each child to make lists sortable with smooth animations out of the box.

sections.title: #Basic usage
sections.content:
To make a list sortable, add wire:sort to the parent element with a handler method name, and wire:sort:item to each child with a unique identifier:
When a user drags and drops an item to a new position, Livewire will call your handler with two parameters: the item's identifier (from wire:sort:item) and the new zero-based position.
You are responsible for persisting the new order in your database.

examples.code: <?php use Livewire\Component; new class extends Component { public TodoList $list;  public function handleSort($id, $position) { $task = $this->list->tasks()->findOrFail($id);  // Update the task's position and re-order other tasks... }};

sections.title: #Sorting across groups
sections.content:
To allow dragging items between multiple lists, use wire:sort:group with the same group name on each container.
To identify which group the item was dropped into, add wire:sort:group-id to each container. Its value will be passed as a third parameter to your handler:
When an item is dragged to a different group, only the destination group's handler fires.

examples.code: <?php use Livewire\Component;use Livewire\Attributes\Computed;use App\Models\Card; new class extends Component { public Board $board;  #[Computed] public function columns() { return $this->board->columns; }  public function handleSort($id, $position, $columnId) { $card = $this->board->cards()->findOrFail($id);  // Update the card's position and re-order other cards... }};

@foreach ($this->columns as $column)
    @foreach ($column->cards as $card)
  • {{ $card->title }}
    • @endforeach
@endforeach

sections.title: #Sort handles
sections.content:
By default, users can drag an item by clicking anywhere on it. To restrict dragging to a specific handle, use wire:sort:handle:
Now users can only initiate a drag from the handle element.

examples.code:
@foreach ($list->tasks as $task)
  •   {{ $task->title }}
    • @endforeach

sections.title: #Ignoring elements
sections.content: To prevent specific areas from triggering drag operations, use wire:sort:ignore. This is useful for buttons or other interactive elements inside sortable items:

examples.code:
@foreach ($list->tasks as $task)
  • {{ $task->title }} 
    • @endforeach
